perturbation.original_text: (b) The Receiving Party agrees that, for a period of eighteen months from the date of this Agreement, the Receiving Party shall not (and shall cause its affiliates, subject to Section 22 hereof, not to), directly or indirectly, (i) use the Confidential Information to divert or attempt to divert any business or customer of the Company or (ii) employ or solicit, or initiate contact for employment with, any (A) director, officer or other senior or key employee of the Company or (B) any other employee of the Company whom the Receiving Party meets during its evaluation of the possible Transaction or about whom the Receiving Party receives Confidential Information; provided , however , a general advertisement or other recruiting efforts not 2 specifically targeting any such employees of the Company shall not be considered a solicitation or unauthorized hiring.
perturbation.changed_text: (b) The Receiving Party agrees that, for a period of eighteen months from the date of this Agreement, the Receiving Party shall not (and shall cause its affiliates, subject to Section 22 hereof, not to), directly or indirectly, (i) use information to divert or attempt to divert any business or customer of the Company or (ii) employ or solicit, or initiate contact for employment with, any (A) director, officer or other senior or key employee of the Company or (B) any other employee of the Company whom the Receiving Party meets during its evaluation of the possible Transaction or about whom the Receiving Party receives information; provided , however , a general advertisement or other recruiting efforts not 2 specifically targeting any such employees of the Company shall not be considered a solicitation or unauthorized hiring.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Confidential Information' to 'information' in Section 3(b), it broadens the scope of restrictions, potentially encompassing publicly available information. However, the agreement still refers to 'Confidential Information' in other sections like Section 2 which defines what is Excluded Information to be considered as Confidential Information. This creates a contradiction because the restrictions now cover a wider range of information than intended by the original agreement's definition of 'Confidential Information'. The contradiction is that the Receiving Party is agreeing to not using information for the purpose of employing or soliciting, when in the original text, that information had to be 'Confidential Information' and due to the definition of 'Confidential Information' now being different, the contract doesn't hold true to its former self.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Excluded Information . The Confidential Information shall not include information that the Receiving Party can demonstrate (a) is or becomes available to the public other than as a result of acts by the Receiving Party or its Representatives in breach of the terms of this Agreement, (b) was in the Receiving Party’s possession prior to disclosure by the Company, provided that such information, to the Receiving Party’s knowledge, was not subject to another confidentiality agreement with the Company or another party, (c) has been independently developed by the Receiving Party without reference to, or the use of, any Confidential Information or (d) is disclosed to the Receiving Party by a third party, to the Receiving Party’s knowledge, not bound by any duty or obligation of confidentiality on a non-confidential basis.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'information' in Section 3(b) broadens the scope of the restriction, potentially encompassing publicly available information. Section 2, however, defines 'Confidential Information' to exclude publicly available information. This creates a direct contradiction. The Receiving Party is now restricted from using a broader set of information, including what is defined as Excluded Information in Section 2, conflicting with the original intent and definition of 'Confidential Information'.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Standstill Agreement . In consideration of the Confidential Information being furnished to the Receiving Party pursuant to this Agreement, the Receiving Party agrees that, for a period of one year from the date of this Agreement (or, such shorter period agreed to by the Company with a third party who is provided access to the Confidential Information for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction, the “ Standstill Period ”), unless expressly requested by the Company or its Board of Directors (or any committee thereof) in writing, the Receiving Party shall not (and shall cause its affiliates not to and shall cause its and their respective Representatives acting at its and their respective behalf not to): (a) in any manner acting alone or in concert with others, acquire, agree to acquire or make any proposal to acquire, directly or indirectly, by means of purchase, merger, business combination or in any other manner, beneficial ownership of any securities of the Company, direct or indirect rights to acquire any securities of the Company (including any derivative securities with economic equivalents of ownership of any of such securities), any right to vote or to direct the voting of any securities of the 3 Company or any assets of the Company, (b) make, or in any way participate in, directly or indirectly, any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) or consents to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person with respect to the voting of, any voting securities of the Company, (c) form, join or in any way participate in a “group” (within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended) with respect to any voting securities of the Company, other than any group comprised solely of the Receiving Party and its affiliates, (d) otherwise act, alone or in concert with others, to seek to control, advise, change or influence the management, board of directors, governing instruments, policies or affairs of the Company, (e) make any public disclosure, or take any action that could require the Company to make any public disclosure, with respect to any of the matters set forth in this Agreement, other than the required amendment to the Receiving Party’s Schedule 13D filing as a result of the execution and delivery of this Agreement, (f) disclose any intention, plan or arrangement inconsistent with the foregoing or (g) have any discussions or enter into any arrangements (whether written or oral) with, or advise, assist or encourage any other persons in connection with any of the foregoing.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Standstill Agreement . In consideration of information being furnished to the Receiving Party pursuant to this Agreement, the Receiving Party agrees that, for a period of one year from the date of this Agreement (or, such shorter period agreed to by the Company with a third party who is provided access to the Confidential Information for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction, the “ Standstill Period ”), unless expressly requested by the Company or its Board of Directors (or any committee thereof) in writing, the Receiving Party shall not (and shall cause its affiliates not to and shall cause its and their respective Representatives acting at its and their respective behalf not to): (a) in any manner acting alone or in concert with others, acquire, agree to acquire or make any proposal to acquire, directly or indirectly, by means of purchase, merger, business combination or in any other manner, beneficial ownership of any securities of the Company, direct or indirect rights to acquire any securities of the Company (including any derivative securities with economic equivalents of ownership of any of such securities), any right to vote or to direct the voting of any securities of the 3 Company or any assets of the Company, (b) make, or in any way participate in, directly or indirectly, any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) or consents to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person with respect to the voting of, any voting securities of the Company, (c) form, join or in any way participate in a “group” (within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended) with respect to any voting securities of the Company, other than any group comprised solely of the Receiving Party and its affiliates, (d) otherwise act, alone or in concert with others, to seek to control, advise, change or influence the management, board of directors, governing instruments, policies or affairs of the Company, (e) make any public disclosure, or take any action that could require the Company to make any public disclosure, with respect to any of the matters set forth in this Agreement, other than the required amendment to the Receiving Party’s Schedule 13D filing as a result of the execution and delivery of this Agreement, (f) disclose any intention, plan or arrangement inconsistent with the foregoing or (g) have any discussions or enter into any arrangements (whether written or oral) with, or advise, assist or encourage any other persons in connection with any of the foregoing.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Confidential Information' to 'information' in the opening sentence of the Standstill Agreement (Section 6), while retaining 'Confidential Information' later in the same sentence (regarding third-party access) creates an internal contradiction. The consideration for the standstill is now based on a broader, undefined 'information,' but the exception for third parties still hinges on 'Confidential Information.' This creates an uncertainty: is the standstill applicable even if the information provided is not considered 'Confidential Information' under the original definition, because the wording is changed for the third party acess?
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Standstill Agreement . In consideration of information being furnished to the Receiving Party pursuant to this Agreement, the Receiving Party agrees that, for a period of one year from the date of this Agreement (or, such shorter period agreed to by the Company with a third party who is provided access to the Confidential Information for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction, the “ Standstill Period ”), unless expressly requested by the Company or its Board of Directors (or any committee thereof) in writing, the Receiving Party shall not (and shall cause its affiliates not to and shall cause its and their respective Representatives acting at its and their respective behalf not to): (a) in any manner acting alone or in concert with others, acquire, agree to acquire or make any proposal to acquire, directly or indirectly, by means of purchase, merger, business combination or in any other manner, beneficial ownership of any securities of the Company, direct or indirect rights to acquire any securities of the Company (including any derivative securities with economic equivalents of ownership of any of such securities), any right to vote or to direct the voting of any securities of the 3 Company or any assets of the Company, (b) make, or in any way participate in, directly or indirectly, any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) or consents to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person with respect to the voting of, any voting securities of the Company, (c) form, join or in any way participate in a “group” (within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended) with respect to any voting securities of the Company, other than any group comprised solely of the Receiving Party and its affiliates, (d) otherwise act, alone or in concert with others, to seek to control, advise, change or influence the management, board of directors, governing instruments, policies or affairs of the Company, (e) make any public disclosure, or take any action that could require the Company to make any public disclosure, with respect to any of the matters set forth in this Agreement, other than the required amendment to the Receiving Party’s Schedule 13D filing as a result of the execution and delivery of this Agreement, (f) disclose any intention, plan or arrangement inconsistent with the foregoing or (g) have any discussions or enter into any arrangements (whether written or oral) with, or advise, assist or encourage any other persons in connection with any of the foregoing.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies the Standstill Agreement is in consideration of 'Confidential Information' being furnished. The changed text broadens this to just 'information.' However, the same sentence continues to refer to 'Confidential Information' when discussing third-party access. This creates an in-text contradiction within Section 6, as the consideration for the standstill is now ambiguous - is it all information or only the defined 'Confidential Information'? This alteration breaks compatibility with the contradicted section.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Return of Confidential Information . Promptly upon the written request of the Company, the Receiving Party will return all copies of the Confidential Information to the Company, and all notes, studies, reports, memoranda and other documents or materials prepared by the Receiving Party or its Representatives that contain or reflect any Confidential Information shall be destroyed. Notwithstanding the return to the Company or destruction of Confidential Information pursuant to this Section 7, the Receiving Party and its Representatives will continue to be bound by their confidentiality obligations and other obligations under this Agreement for the term 4 hereof. Notwithstanding the foregoing, (a) the Receiving Party’s legal department and/or outside counsel may keep one copy of the Confidential Information (in electronic or paper form) and, with respect to the Receiving Party’s Representatives who are accounting firms, such firms may keep one copy of the Confidential Information, in each case, if required to comply with applicable law or regulation and (b) the Receiving Party and its Representatives may retain Confidential Information to the extent it is “backed-up” on its or their (as the case may be) electronic information management and communications systems or servers, is not available to an end user and cannot be expunged without considerable effort; provided, that any such information so retained pursuant to clauses (a) and (b) of this Section 7 shall be held in compliance with the terms of this Agreement for a period of eighteen months from the date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Return of Confidential Information . Promptly upon the written request of the Company, the Receiving Party will return all copies of the Information to the Company, and all notes, studies, reports, memoranda and other documents or materials prepared by the Receiving Party or its Representatives that contain or reflect any Information shall be destroyed. Notwithstanding the return to the Company or destruction of Information pursuant to this Section 7, the Receiving Party and its Representatives will continue to be bound by their confidentiality obligations and other obligations under this Agreement for the term 4 hereof. Notwithstanding the foregoing, (a) the Receiving Party’s legal department and/or outside counsel may keep one copy of the Information (in electronic or paper form) and, with respect to the Receiving Party’s Representatives who are accounting firms, such firms may keep one copy of the Information, in each case, if required to comply with applicable law or regulation and (b) the Receiving Party and its Representatives may retain Information to the extent it is “backed-up” on its or their (as the case may be) electronic information management and communications systems or servers, is not available to an end user and cannot be expunged without considerable effort; provided, that any such information so retained pursuant to clauses (a) and (b) of this Section 7 shall be held in compliance with the terms of this Agreement for a period of eighteen months from the date hereof.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Information' throughout section 7, the section contradicts itself in regard to what information it wants returned to it. The Receiving party must return all 'information' to the Company, however, only has confidentiality obligations and must retain information in compliance with the confidentiality agreement. The use of information gives the impression to outside information or public information, so it shouldn't be held to any confidentiality agreements
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Return of Confidential Information . Promptly upon the written request of the Company, the Receiving Party will return all copies of the Information to the Company, and all notes, studies, reports, memoranda and other documents or materials prepared by the Receiving Party or its Representatives that contain or reflect any Information shall be destroyed. Notwithstanding the return to the Company or destruction of Information pursuant to this Section 7, the Receiving Party and its Representatives will continue to be bound by their confidentiality obligations and other obligations under this Agreement for the term 4 hereof. Notwithstanding the foregoing, (a) the Receiving Party’s legal department and/or outside counsel may keep one copy of the Information (in electronic or paper form) and, with respect to the Receiving Party’s Representatives who are accounting firms, such firms may keep one copy of the Information, in each case, if required to comply with applicable law or regulation and (b) the Receiving Party and its Representatives may retain Information to the extent it is “backed-up” on its or their (as the case may be) electronic information management and communications systems or servers, is not available to an end user and cannot be expunged without considerable effort; provided, that any such information so retained pursuant to clauses (a) and (b) of this Section 7 shall be held in compliance with the terms of this Agreement for a period of eighteen months from the date hereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information,' implying a specific subset of information subject to confidentiality obligations. The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Information' throughout Section 7. This change creates a contradiction because Section 7 discusses the return and destruction of 'Information' while also referencing confidentiality obligations and exceptions that should only apply to 'Confidential Information.' Using 'Information' broadly implies that all information, even publicly available information, is subject to these obligations, which is logically inconsistent and contradicts the intended scope of the confidentiality agreement. Therefore, there is a strong in-text contradiction as the definition of 'Information' now conflicts with the intended scope of the agreement elsewhere in the same section, as 'Information' might include public information but is still beholden to the confidentiality agreement.

perturbation.original_text: 12. Communications . Without the Company’s prior written consent, which may be withheld by the Company in its sole discretion, the Receiving Party shall not (and shall cause its Representatives not to) initiate, other than through the Company’s financial and legal advisors or such other persons, as designated by the Company in writing, any (a) communication concerning the Confidential Information, (b) requests for meetings with management of the Company in connection with the possible Transaction or other transaction between the parties or (c) communication relating to the business of the Company or the possible Transaction, in each case, with any officer, director or employee of the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: 12. Communications . Without the Company’s prior written consent, which may be withheld by the Company in its sole discretion, the Receiving Party shall not (and shall cause its Representatives not to) initiate, other than through the Company’s financial and legal advisors or such other persons, as designated by the Company in writing, any (a) communication, (b) requests for meetings with management of the Company in connection with the possible Transaction or other transaction between the parties or (c) communication relating to the business of the Company or the possible Transaction, in each case, with any officer, director or employee of the Company.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the words “concerning the Confidential Information” in Section 12(a), the provision now prohibits *any* communication with the Company, regardless of its content. This contradicts the overall purpose of the agreement, which is to facilitate a possible transaction through the exchange of information. Blocking all communication is contradictory with the intention that the Parties are to communicate with one another.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Confidential Information; Representatives . (a) The Receiving Party is considering a possible business combination transaction (whether negotiated directly with the Company’s Board of Directors or otherwise) with respect to the Company (the “ Transaction ”). As a condition to the Confidential Information being furnished to the Receiving Party and the directors, officers, principals, partners, members, employees, agents, consultants, related investment funds, advisors, attorneys, accountants, affiliates, potential sources of capital and financing, and financial advisors (collectively, “ Representatives ”) of the Receiving Party, the Receiving Party agrees to treat the Confidential Information strictly in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement and to otherwise comply, and to cause its Representatives to comply, with all obligations hereinafter set forth.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 12(a) omits "concerning the Confidential Information," broadening the communication restriction to *any* communication. This directly contradicts Section 1, which states the purpose of the agreement is to facilitate a possible transaction through the exchange of Confidential Information. Restricting *all* communication is incompatible with the intention that the Parties are to communicate regarding the possible transaction, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction. The modified section is not compatible with Section 1, causing a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 22. Non-Private Equity Affiliates . Notwithstanding anything to the contrary provided elsewhere herein, none of the provisions of this Agreement shall in any way limit the ordinary course business services and activities of the Receiving Party and its affiliates; provided, that (a) such services and activities are distinct from the private equity business and (b) the Confidential Information is not made available to Representatives of the Receiving Party and its affiliates who are not involved in the private equity business or who are engaged in investments that are not private equity investments and such ordinary course business services and activities are otherwise conducted without any reference to, or use of, the Confidential Information. The Receiving Party further represents to the Company that the Receiving Party has implemented appropriate internal restrictions on the sharing of confidential information (including, without limitation, the implementation of ethical walls around certain affiliates, to comply with federal securities laws of the United States).
perturbation.changed_text: 22. Non-Private Equity Affiliates . Notwithstanding anything to the contrary provided elsewhere herein, none of the provisions of this Agreement shall in any way limit the ordinary course business services and activities of the Receiving Party and its affiliates; provided, that (a) such services and activities are distinct from the private equity business and (b) information is not made available to Representatives of the Receiving Party and its affiliates who are not involved in the private equity business or who are engaged in investments that are not private equity investments and such ordinary course business services and activities are otherwise conducted without any reference to, or use of, information. The Receiving Party further represents to the Company that the Receiving Party has implemented appropriate internal restrictions on the sharing of confidential information (including, without limitation, the implementation of ethical walls around certain affiliates, to comply with federal securities laws of the United States).
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Confidential Information' to 'information' in Section 22(b), it expands the scope of restricted information shared with non-private equity affiliates. Now any information is not to be shared, so this creates the need for an ethical wall around certain affiliates. But in this section it goes on to say that ethical walls were created to comply with federal securities laws of the United States. It makes it seem contradictory that you would need to create an ethical wall around any information to comply with those laws.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 22. Non-Private Equity Affiliates . Notwithstanding anything to the contrary provided elsewhere herein, none of the provisions of this Agreement shall in any way limit the ordinary course business services and activities of the Receiving Party and its affiliates; provided, that (a) such services and activities are distinct from the private equity business and (b) information is not made available to Representatives of the Receiving Party and its affiliates who are not involved in the private equity business or who are engaged in investments that are not private equity investments and such ordinary course business services and activities are otherwise conducted without any reference to, or use of, information. The Receiving Party further represents to the Company that the Receiving Party has implemented appropriate internal restrictions on the sharing of confidential information (including, without limitation, the implementation of ethical walls around certain affiliates, to comply with federal securities laws of the United States).
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'information' in Section 22(b) broadens the scope of restricted information. The Perturbation Explanation states this creates the need for an ethical wall around certain affiliates. The original text implies ethical walls are solely for complying with federal securities laws related to 'Confidential Information'. The changed text coupled with the Contradicted Text now requires an ethical wall for *any* information, creating an inconsistency in reasoning. This leads to a contradiction because it is no longer clear if the ethical wall implementation extends to all information or just confidential information as previously indicated.
